abstracts.abstract:
Existing law establishes the Department of Veterans Affairs, which administers various programs that provide benefits to veterans, as defined, including educational assistance and farm and home purchase assistance.

Existing law establishes the Department of Motor Vehicles in the Transportation Agency and sets forth various powers and duties for the department, including prescribing and providing suitable forms of applications, certificates of ownership, registration cards, driver's licenses, and all other forms that are deemed necessary.

This bill would require the Department of Motor Vehicles, in coordination with the Department of Veterans Affairs, to provide mobile services to each veteran resident at each California veterans home at least once per calendar year. The bill would require the Department of Veterans Affairs to assist by coordinating the scheduling, space, and outreach to residents to facilitate the provision of those services. For the purposes of these provisions, the bill would define "mobile services" as, among other things, vehicle registration services and Real ID application processing.

other_titles.title: State educational entities: Educational Innovation and Planning Commission.

actions.description: From printer. May be heard in committee March 23.
From committee chair, with author's amendments: Amend, and re-refer to Com. on ED. Read second time and amended.

extras.impact_clause: An act to add Article 3 (commencing with Section 983) to Chapter 6 of Division 4 of the Military and Veterans Code, relating to veterans.
